parsed_review.summary:
CP00-682 (RP22 External Integrations I, P06 scope/contract/implementation/fixture bridge descriptor; risk C, 150 units, RP22.P06.M00.S07-RP22.P06.M07.S06) passes hardened read-only review. The pack's headline claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / status pending_review — i.e. an explicitly descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, runtime-closed bridge — and that claim is fully supported by the evidence I inspected directly via Read/Grep/Glob.

Verified: (1) Descriptor-only/no-write — no fetch/http/fs-write/exec/spawn/child_process in packages/integrations-core/src; freezeCp682Result (service.js:489) hard-codes ~25 no-runtime/no-write/no-real-data flags to false/true (writes_product_state:false, calls_external_provider_api:false, persists_access/refresh_token:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, includes_credential/secret_value:false, leak_detected:false). (2) Validators are substantive, not trivial — validateExternalIntegrationsICp682P06...Descriptor (validators.js:1765) asserts every row descriptor_only / no runtime_execution / customer-safe / no raw payload, all 16 contract runtime flags false, and authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval false; the projection validator pins current_pack=CP00-682 and re-validates CP666–682 descriptors, requirements, and no-write attestations. (3) Tests exercise the claims — model.test.js:438 (coverage matches live plan distribution) and :806 (descriptor bridges P06 rows with all runtime flags false, claude read_only/non-final, handoff to CP00-683/RP22.P06.M07.S07). (4) Coverage matches baseline exactly: 150 units, deliverable distribution {implementation:56, security_audit:35, ui:28, claude_review:7, test:24} and micro-title distribution all match registry requirements and the prompt baseline. (5) No secrets/PII in implementation or contracts; external contract carries 985 synthetic_only/no-real-data attestations. (6) Authority boundary intact and matches the prompt: Claude is read_only_independent_reviewer and not final approver; Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor-only review-packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_opened:false, runtime_receipt_emitted:false) — not product runtime. (7) Readiness auth-preflight meets CP178+ hardening: exit 0, 1445-byte stdout (non-zero), valid JSON result, no auth failure. (8) CP00-682 is live_latest_pack_id with CP00-683 queued at RP22.P06.M07.S07; handoff is consistent. The validate script writes contracts only with --write, so the validation runs are read-only.

Provenance caveat: the 22 command exit codes in command-evidence.json (incl. npm test, npm run validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp22 validate, git diff --check, build) are recorded evidence — I did not re-execute them (read-only, no Bash). I independently confirmed the source, validators, tests, and contracts directly support every claim, so the recorded greens are consistent with the code. blocks_goal_closeout=false: the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (enterprise trust closed outside this receipt, pending human approval as designed), so the human-approval boundary does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
